Just across the much visited Ghirardelli Square is a smallish park which is beloved by locals and tourists alike. What makes it unique is its location. It is surrounded by what tourists like to do and where most of them go in San Francisco. Aside from Ghirardelli Square, it is along the route of the cable car, a stone's throw away from the crooked Lombard Street, walking distance to Fisherman's Wharf and the north side of Little Italy, and most of all, it has the scenic views of Russian Hill where it is located. The park has landscaped lawn and garden, and play ground. When you need a breather from visiting all the surrounding attractions, this is the perfect location where you can rest and absorb all the beautiful scenery around you. Before it was built in 2022, I used to go to this vacant lot atop Larkin Street because of the breathtaking view of Golden Gate Bridge and Alcatraz. Now this empty lot on a former reservoir has been transformed. It's amazing what they have done to this place.

A delicious experience for those who love chocolate! It is very worth tasting the hot chocolate and various options from the cafeteria. 
We were able to go early in the morning and there were no queues.
We had an amazing chocolate experience at Ghirardelli. We all had different sundaes. They also have a dairy-alternative, which is awesome for our child. Pricey? Yes, worth it? Absolutely!
